records.ety: [F. surprise, fr. surprendre, surpris; sur over + prendre to take, L. prehendere. See Sur-, and Prehensile.]

defs: The act of coming upon, or taking, unawares; the act of seizing unexpectedly; surprisal; as, the fort was taken by surprise.

The state of being surprised, or taken unawares, by some act or event which could not reasonably be foreseen; emotion excited by what is sudden and strange; a suddenly excited feeling of wonder or astonishment.
quotes: Pure surprise and fear Made me to quit the house.

defs: Anything that causes such a state or emotion.
A dish covered with a crust of raised paste, but with no other contents.

records.ety: [From Surprise, n.: cf. F. surprendre, p. p. surpris.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Surprised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Surprising.]

defs: To come or fall suddenly and unexpectedly; to take unawares; to seize or capture by unexpected attack.
quotes: The castle of Macduff I will surprise.
Who can speak The mingled passions that surprised his heart?

defs: To strike with wonder, astonishment, or confusion, by something sudden, unexpected, or remarkable; to confound; as, his conduct surprised me.
quotes: I am surprised with an uncouth fear.
Up he starts, Discovered and surprised.

defs: To lead (one) to do suddenly and without forethought; to bring (one) into some unexpected state; -- with into; as, to be surprised into an indiscretion; to be surprised into generosity.
To hold possession of; to hold.
quotes: Not with me, That in my hands surprise the sovereignity.
